backstory: Solo founder, 18 months in, building a B2B SaaS on Next.js. Stripe live for 6 months, ~$4k MRR. Plays with Linear but not seriously. Wants to look like a real engineering org without being one.
expected_wow: Two-stage wow. (1) Four-pillar initial sweep at turn 2: 'What you're building' surfaces B2B SaaS framing; 'Who's working on it with you' surfaces solo + forward-looking note about Stripe customers + Linear team post-connect; 'What we noticed' surfaces 3-5 grounded sub-CTAs (security risk on payment surface, stale PR, TODO); 'What we can connect to' surfaces gh + Stripe CLI + Sentry SDK; primary CTA = connect-deeper. (2) Plan menu post-connect at turn 4 with deeper rescan data ('47 customers on Stripe, 3 churned this month'). User picks based on what they need TODAY.
expected_dropoff_risk: OAuth fatigue at /mur connect github (turn 3). Solo founder is busy. The four-pillar sweep should land hard enough at turn 2 that the connect-deeper ask feels earned, not asked-too-soon.
wow_score_target: ≥ 3 in H3 (wow before ask), H6 (sweep at turn 2), H8 (chief-of-staff voice), H9 (post-connect menu breadth), H10 (menu items grounded), H11 (all four pillars render), H12 (single primary CTA), H13 (pre/post-connect separation)
scoring_notes: Richest signals (Stripe + GH + Sentry + OpenAI). Four-pillar sweep should populate all four pillars; post-connect menu should fire 4-5 grounded options. Watch H12: scan output must NOT include a predictive digest preview — that's gone. Watch H13: external data ('customers across Stripe') only appears post-connect.
